entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: Cultivated chickpea varieties used as susceptible checks in the study

entities.name: WILD RELATIVES OF CHICKPEA
entities.description: Wild species of Cicer including C. bijugum, C. reticulatum, C. cuneatum, C. chrossanicum, C. judaicum, and C. microphyllum

entities.name: HELICOVERPA ARMIGERA
entities.description: A lepidopteran pest that causes damage to chickpea pods and leaves

entities.name: RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of chickpea genotypes to withstand or repel attack by H. armigera

entities.name: ANTIXENOSIS
entities.description: The non-preference of H. armigera for oviposition on certain chickpea genotypes

entities.name: ANTIBIOSIS
entities.description: The adverse effect of host plant genotypes on the survival, development, and fecundity of H. armigera

entities.name: LEAF DAMAGE RATING
entities.description: A measure of the visual damage caused by H. armigera larvae on leaves

entities.name: POD DAMAGE
entities.description: A measure of the percentage of pods damaged by H. armigera larvae

entities.name: LARVAL SURVIVAL
entities.description: The percentage of H. armigera larvae that survive on a host plant

entities.name: PUPATION
entities.description: The rate of larval transformation into pupae

entities.name: ADULT EMERGENCE
entities.description: The rate of pupal transformation into adult moths

entities.name: FECUNDITY
entities.description: The number of eggs produced by adult H. armigera

entities.name: FIELD CONDITIONS
entities.description: Evaluation of pest damage and resistance under natural field environments

entities.name: DETACHED LEAF ASSAY
entities.description: A laboratory method to evaluate larval damage and weight on separated leaves

entities.name: DETACHED POD ASSAY
entities.description: A laboratory method to evaluate larval damage and weight on separated pods

entities.name: ARTIFICIAL DIET ASSAY
entities.description: A method using artificial diet impregnated with lyophilized leaf powders to study antibiosis

entities.name: ICRISAT
entities.description: International Crops Research Institute for the Semi-Arid Tropics, Patancheru

entities.name: BIOCHEMICAL MECHANISMS
entities.description: Chemical processes within the plant that contribute to pest resistance

entities.name: MOLECULAR MECHANISMS
entities.description: Genetic and molecular processes within the plant that contribute to pest resistance

relationships.description: Exhibit higher levels of resistance compared to cultivated chickpea

Show high levels of antibiosis affecting pest development
Reduces the survival rate of larvae
Reduces the percentage of adult emergence
Reduces the fecundity of adult insects
Used to evaluate resistance via leaf and pod damage
Used to evaluate resistance via larval weight and damage
Used to evaluate resistance via pod damage and larval weight
